deadlines.notes: RFP timeline table says April 7, 2026 12 P.M. PDT. The cover page also says April 7, 2026 12 P.M. Pacific Time. However, Section VI (Deadline and Proposal Submission) in the RFP body states 'Proposals are due no later than 12:00 P.M. Pacific Time (PT) on Tuesday, May 5, 2026.' The OpenGov portal listing shows April 7, 2026 as the due date. There is a discrepancy between the RFP timeline (April 7) and Section VI text (May 5). The OpenGov portal and RFP timeline table both confirm April 7, 2026. May 5, 2026 is listed as the Interviews date in the timeline table, suggesting the May 5 date in Section VI may be a typographical error.

warnings: Discrepancy found: RFP cover page, timeline table, and OpenGov portal all state proposals due April 7, 2026, but Section VI of the RFP body states May 5, 2026. Using April 7, 2026 as the primary deadline based on multiple corroborating sources. May 5 appears in the timeline as the Interviews date, suggesting a possible typographical error in Section VI.

data.basisOfEstimate: No meaningful price estimate can be developed because this solicitation represents a fundamental industry mismatch. The RFP seeks a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) cloud-based project management software product designed for construction project tracking, capital improvement planning, cost control, scheduling, RFIs, submittals, and change orders at airport facilities. The competitive landscape for this procurement includes established vendors like Procore, e-Builder (Trimble), Kahua, PMWeb, and similar construction PM platforms with decades of public-sector deployment history. Horus Technology is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner specializing in AI/ML, document automation, and cloud-native development — it does not offer a project management software product. The organization's provided labor rates (Manufacturing Engineer $178.89/hr, QA $16.86/hr, Software Engineer $171.40/hr) and BOM items are not applicable to this procurement. No government estimated value was provided in the solicitation. The evaluation is best-value with Fee Schedule weighted at 20 of 100 points, alongside Service Support Capabilities (20), Implementation Plan (20), Company Qualifications (15), and Relevant Public Sector Experience (15). Even if pricing were competitive, Horus would fail the Pass/Fail minimum qualifications gate requiring 10 years of continuous PM software experience with public agencies. Total price is set to $0 as the company fundamentally cannot perform this work as described.
assumptions: Horus Technology does not currently offer or resell a commercial project management software product suitable for construction and capital improvement management
Horus Technology does not have 10 years of continuous experience providing project management software services to public agencies, which is a Pass/Fail minimum qualification
No government estimated value was provided in the solicitation documents
Organization labor rates provided (Manufacturing Engineer, QA, Software Engineer) are not applicable to this COTS software procurement

The competitive field for this RFP consists of established construction PM software vendors (Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, PMWeb, Oracle Primavera, etc.) with mature SaaS products and extensive public-sector track records

The contract type is anticipated to be a negotiated agreement covering software licensing fees, implementation services, training, and ongoing support — likely structured as annual subscription plus professional services

The 8-year maximum term (4-year base + two 2-year options) implies total contract value likely in the range of $500K-$2M+ depending on licensing model, but this is speculative market intelligence, not a Horus-specific estimate
Price confidence is set to 10 (lowest tier) due to complete industry mismatch, no government estimate, and inapplicable org rates
pricingRisks: CRITICAL DISQUALIFIER: RFP requires minimum 10 years of continuous experience providing project management software services to public agencies — Horus Technology does not appear to meet this minimum qualification

This RFP seeks a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) project management software product (e.g., Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, PMWeb) for construction and capital improvement management — Horus Technology is an AWS consulting/AI/ML firm and does not offer such a product
The solicitation requires demonstrated capability in large public-use facilities such as airports or correctional facilities — no evidence Horus has this experience
Scope requires unlimited software licensing, ongoing technical support, software updates, and security patches over up to 8 years — this implies a mature SaaS product, not custom development
FAA General Contract Provisions apply, adding regulatory compliance burden specific to airport operations
Sealed bid process with pricing sealed separately means no opportunity to adjust pricing based on competitor visibility

8-year total contract duration (4-year base + two 2-year options) requires long-term product sustainability and support infrastructure that a custom-built solution would struggle to provide cost-effectively
Integration requirements with ERP, GIS, SSO, and document repositories require a mature product with established connectors — not a greenfield development effort
Background check and airport security badge requirements for on-site staff add operational complexity
recommendedActions: STRONG NO-BID RECOMMENDATION: Horus Technology does not meet the minimum qualifications (10 years providing PM software to public agencies) and does not offer a COTS project management software product — this solicitation should not be pursued

If interested in the airport/public infrastructure market long-term, consider partnering as a technology subcontractor to an established PM software vendor (e.g., Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, PMWeb) for integration or customization work
Monitor Sacramento County Department of Airports for future IT modernization, cloud migration, or AI/ML opportunities that align with Horus's core AWS consulting capabilities
Attend the optional pre-proposal conference on February 20, 2026 only if exploring a teaming arrangement with an established PM software vendor
Leverage California DGS Small Business / Micro Business certification for future Sacramento County opportunities that match Horus's actual service offerings

evaluationFactors: Relevant Public Sector Experience and References (15 points)
Highest-ranked proposers may be required to attend a virtual interview which will serve as the basis for the panel's final decision
data.overview: Sacramento County Department of Airports seeks a qualified vendor to provide a comprehensive, fully supported cloud-based Project Management Software solution including implementation, customization, training, and ongoing support. The software must support construction project management and Capital Improvement Planning (CIP) across four airports, replacing current Excel-based tracking. The initial agreement term is four years with two two-year extension options (up to eight years total). Success means the Department achieves self-sufficiency in using the software for project tracking, scheduling, budgeting, reporting, and CIP processes.

requirements.requirement: Provide comprehensive cloud-based Project Management Software for project tracking, schedules, and maintaining project budgets with reporting capabilities
Software must support Capital Improvement Planning (CIP) process including portfolio-level visibility, prioritization, and long-term forecasting

Provide unlimited software licenses for all County staff, consultants, and external stakeholders requiring access, including full access to all modules, features, dashboards, workflow tools, and administrative functions
Licenses must permit concurrent users, remote access, and ability to scale as County operations grow
Complete installation of Project Management Software including all server components, databases, system integrations, and user environment configuration

Configure system for project status/updating reporting, capital planning, project initiation, project budget and cost tracking, invoice processing, schedule management, change orders, RFIs, submittals, and reporting dashboards and alerts
Provide automated workflows for RFI, submittals, change orders, and approvals
Provide real-time dashboards and alerts for cost and schedule control
Customize software to align with Department-specific project management and approval workflows including configuring system fields, forms, dashboards, and approval routing

Support portfolio and capital planning tools, document control, budget and cost module customization, schedule management visibility, audit trails and version tracking, and automated alerting and notifications
All customization must remain supportable, upgradable, and compatible with future software versions

Provide integration with existing County systems including ERP or financial systems, document repositories, Single Sign-On (SSO), GIS, scheduling tools, or other platforms as identified during implementation
Supply all API documentation, middleware, and technical support needed for successful integration

Develop up to ten (10) customized reports tailored to County needs including capital project status, portfolio summary, budget and expenditure tracking, schedule progress dashboard, contract and change order reporting, and executive-level project status rollup
Reports must be exportable to standard formats (PDF, Excel, CSV) and configurable by County administrators

Provide full-service ongoing technical support including helpdesk availability, software updates and system patches, troubleshooting assistance, and resolution of system issues and performance concerns
Include access to all scheduled software upgrades, enhancements, and security releases
Provide a dedicated technical representative during implementation and early adoption phases

Work with County IT team to meet all security, hosting, and technology compliance requirements including authentication and user permissions, system performance standards, integration capabilities (API availability, data import/export), and data retention and backup protocols
Ability to meet the County of Sacramento Security Requirements as described in Attachment 5
Staff working on-site unescorted or requiring Airport access must pass Department background check and obtain an airport access badge
Provide up to six (6) in-person administrator training sessions
Provide up to ten (10) combination of in-person and virtual end-user training sessions
Provide up to six (6) virtual train-the-trainer sessions
Furnish user manuals, quick reference guides, and recorded training modules
Deliver a training plan that addresses usability considerations
Provide system architecture and configuration documentation
Provide data model and integration specifications
Provide security protocols and compliance documentation
Provide administrator handbook and technical references
Develop complete implementation plan including timeline and milestones, roles and responsibilities, data migration plan, testing and validation procedures, and change management strategy
Contractor must be registered with the California Secretary of State and permitted to conduct business in the State of California
Contractor shall maintain insurance and workers' compensation coverage that complies with or exceeds the minimum requirements established by the County
Comply with FAA General Contract Provisions for Solicitations (Attachment 2)
Complete County of Sacramento Contractor Certification of Compliance Form (Attachment 3)
Complete Contractor Identification Form (Attachment 4)
Provide Iran Contracting Act Certification per California Public Contract Code sections 2202-2208
Certify Independent Contractor Status via OpenGov questionnaire
Proposer must possess a minimum of ten (10) years of continuous experience in providing project management software services to public agencies
Demonstrate capability to provide services in a large public use facility in continuous operation such as an airport or similar facility
Provide references from at least three (3) clients or individuals with direct experience within the last five (5) years
Include balance sheet and income statements for the last two fiscal years prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles
Provide proposed Fee Schedule for the initial four-year term and for each of the additional two-year extension options
Include hourly rates, overhead multipliers or other fees, anticipated hours for all employees proposed, and justification of hours to complete required scope

format: One (1) digital PDF copy of the proposal compatible with Adobe Acrobat
One (1) digital copy of the proposal compatible with Microsoft Word in DOC or DOCX format
Proposals must be typed or printed in a font no smaller than eleven (11) point on 8.5 by 11 inch pages
Proposal must not exceed forty (40) pages including any marketing materials

Items excluded from page limit: cover page, cover letter, staff resumes, contractor team organization chart, independent market study, Contractor Certification of Compliance Form, and Contractor Identification Form
Proposal must be signed by an authorized employee or officer
Sealed Bid Process: Bids Sealed / Pricing Sealed
Cost proposal must be uploaded separately from the main proposal

requiredVolumes: Fee Structure, Proposal Cost, and Scope (Section XI)

deliverables: Cloud-based Project Management Software with unlimited licenses
Software customization aligned with Department workflows
System integration with ERP, document repositories, SSO, GIS, and other County systems
Up to six (6) in-person administrator training sessions
Up to ten (10) combination in-person and virtual end-user training sessions
Up to six (6) virtual train-the-trainer sessions
User manuals, quick reference guides, and recorded training modules
Complete implementation plan with timeline, milestones, roles, data migration plan, testing and validation procedures, and change management strategy
Ongoing technical support including helpdesk, updates, patches, and dedicated technical representative

redFlags.whyItMatters: The solicitation requires 10 years of continuous experience and demonstrated capability at large public facilities. No relevant past performance was found in the company's records, raising serious eligibility and competitiveness concerns.
redFlags.mitigation: Conduct an internal audit of all prior engagements. If the 10-year experience threshold cannot be met, this should be treated as a strong no-bid indicator unless a qualified teaming partner can be identified.
redFlags.flag: No past performance found for HORUSTECH in project management software services to public agencies

redFlags.whyItMatters: The OpenGov portal indicates this is a sealed bid process with pricing sealed separately. Fee Schedule is worth 20 points, but technical factors (Service Support 20pts, Implementation Plan 20pts, Company Qualifications 15pts, Experience 15pts) total 70 points. A firm without strong public sector PM software experience cannot overcome technical scoring gaps through pricing alone.
redFlags.mitigation: Honestly assess technical scoring potential against the 70-point technical criteria before investing in proposal development. Focus proposal effort on the highest-weighted areas if proceeding.
redFlags.flag: Sealed bid process with separate cost proposal — pricing cannot compensate for technical gaps

risks.whyItMatters: Section IX.C states 'The Proposer must possess a minimum of ten (10) years of continuous experience in providing project management software services to public agencies.' This is a pass/fail minimum qualification — proposals that do not meet it will not be evaluated. No past performance records were found in HORUSTECH's knowledge base to confirm this threshold is met.
risks.mitigation: Verify HORUSTECH has 10+ years of continuous experience providing PM software to public agencies. If not, consider teaming with a firm that does, or assess whether this is a no-bid. Document all qualifying engagements with dates and client names.
risks.flag: Minimum 10 years continuous experience in project management software services to public agencies required

risks.whyItMatters: Section X states the Contractor must 'successfully demonstrate the capability to provide the services described in this RFP in a large public use facility in continuous operation, such as in an airport or similar such as a correctional facility.' No relevant past performance was found in HORUSTECH's records for airport or similar facility engagements.
risks.mitigation: Identify and document any prior engagements at airports, transit authorities, correctional facilities, or similar large public-use facilities. If none exist, this is a significant competitive weakness and potential no-bid factor.
risks.flag: Demonstrated capability in large public use facility (airport or similar) required

risks.whyItMatters: Section XI.A requires submission of balance sheets and income statements for the last two fiscal years prepared in accordance with GAAP, plus interim statements for significant financial events. This is a substantive disclosure requirement that may be burdensome for smaller firms.
risks.mitigation: Prepare GAAP-compliant financial statements well in advance. Engage an accountant if statements are not already in GAAP format. Ensure interim statements are ready if any significant financial events have occurred.
risks.flag: Financial disclosure requirement — two years of balance sheets and income statements

risks.whyItMatters: Section XVII.A and Attachment 2 require compliance with FAA General Contract Provisions for Solicitations throughout the agreement term. These provisions may include DBE requirements, Buy American provisions, and other federal obligations that add compliance complexity.
risks.mitigation: Review Attachment 2 (FAA Exhibits) thoroughly to identify all federal compliance obligations. Assess whether the firm can meet DBE, Buy American, and other FAA requirements. Build compliance costs into the fee schedule.
risks.flag: FAA General Contract Provisions compliance required

risks.whyItMatters: Section XVI requires staff working on-site unescorted to pass the Department's background check and obtain an airport access badge. This could delay implementation if key personnel fail background checks.
risks.mitigation: Identify all staff who may need on-site access early. Pre-screen personnel for any issues that could prevent passing airport background checks. Have backup personnel identified.
risks.flag: Airport security background checks and badge requirements for on-site staff

risks.whyItMatters: Section VIII.D limits proposals to 40 pages including marketing materials (with some exclusions). Given the breadth of the scope (licensing, implementation, customization, integration, training, support, reporting, documentation), fitting a comprehensive response within 40 pages requires careful planning.
risks.mitigation: Create a detailed outline early mapping each evaluation criterion to page allocations. Prioritize high-weighted categories (Service Support Capabilities 20pts, Implementation Plan 20pts, Fee Schedule 20pts). Leverage excluded items (resumes, org chart) to free space.
risks.flag: 40-page proposal limit with detailed scope requirements

risks.whyItMatters: Section IX.A requires the Contractor to be registered with the California Secretary of State and permitted to conduct business in California. This is a pass/fail minimum qualification.
risks.mitigation: Verify current registration status with California Secretary of State. If not registered, initiate the process immediately as it may take several weeks.
risks.flag: California Secretary of State registration required

risks.whyItMatters: Section XIV states 'The highest-ranked proposers may be required to attend a virtual interview, which will serve as the basis for the panel's final decision.' Interviews are scheduled for May 5, 2026, meaning the interview could override written proposal scores.
risks.mitigation: Prepare key personnel for virtual interview presentations. Develop talking points aligned with the highest-weighted evaluation criteria. Conduct mock interviews before the scheduled date.
risks.flag: Potential interviews required for highest-ranked proposers

incumbentInfo.notes: The solicitation states in Section X.A.2 that 'The Department does not currently use a project management system' and currently relies on Excel spreadsheets and Crystal Reports. This is a new capability acquisition, not a recompete of an existing contract.

data.confidenceExplanation: Confidence is 55 due to: no past performance data available (-20), fundamental industry mismatch between company capabilities and solicitation requirements (-15), and expired submission deadline which is independently disqualifying (-10). The analysis is high-confidence in the NO_GO recommendation despite low overall data confidence because the disqualifiers are unambiguous and absolute.
requiredActions: Do not pursue this solicitation — it is expired and fundamentally misaligned with company capabilities
Monitor Sacramento County Department of Airports for future IT modernization, cloud migration, or AI/ML opportunities that align with Horus's core AWS consulting capabilities

If interested in the airport/public infrastructure market long-term, explore teaming as a technology subcontractor to established PM software vendors for integration or customization work on future opportunities
Leverage California DGS Small Business / Micro Business certification for future Sacramento County opportunities matching Horus's actual service offerings

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: Horus Technology is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner specializing in AI/ML, document automation, and cloud-native development. The solicitation requires a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) cloud-based project management software product for construction project tracking, capital improvement planning, RFIs, submittals, change orders, cost control, and scheduling at airport facilities. Horus does not offer a COTS PM software product (e.g., Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, PMWeb). The company's capabilities in document intelligence and AI/ML are fundamentally misaligned with the requirement for a mature construction project management platform with unlimited licensing, ongoing SaaS support, and established integrations with ERP, GIS, and SSO systems. Additionally, the RFP requires a minimum of 10 years of continuous experience providing PM software services to public agencies — a pass/fail disqualifier that Horus cannot meet.
gaps: No COTS project management software product for construction/CIP
No 10 years of continuous experience providing PM software to public agencies
No experience with construction workflows (RFIs, submittals, change orders, cost control)
No demonstrated capability at airports or similar large public-use facilities
No ERP, GIS, or construction-specific system integration experience
No SaaS licensing model with unlimited concurrent users
No ongoing helpdesk, patching, and upgrade infrastructure for a PM software product

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No past performance data available. Zero matched projects were found in the company database for project management software, construction management, capital improvement planning, airport operations, or public agency PM software services. The company's documented past performance (e.g., Avue Technologies federal HR platform work) is in document automation and AI/ML — completely unrelated to the construction PM software domain required by this solicitation. The RFP requires three references from clients with direct experience in the last five years and demonstrated capability at large public-use facilities like airports. Horus cannot satisfy these requirements.
gaps: No past performance in project management software services
No past performance with public agencies for PM software
No past performance at airports or similar large public-use facilities
No references available for construction PM software implementations
Cannot meet the three-reference requirement for PM software services

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: Pricing analysis confirms a fundamental industry mismatch with price confidence of 10 (lowest tier). Horus does not offer a COTS PM software product, so it cannot price software licensing, SaaS subscriptions, or ongoing support competitively against established vendors like Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, or PMWeb. The company's labor rates (Manufacturing Engineer $178.89/hr, Software Engineer $171.40/hr) are inapplicable to this COTS software procurement. Total price was set to $0 by the pricing module because the company fundamentally cannot perform this work. Competitive position rated HIGH risk. Even if a custom solution were attempted, it would be orders of magnitude more expensive than established SaaS products and would not meet the maturity, scalability, and long-term support requirements of an 8-year contract.
gaps: No software product to license or price
Labor rates inapplicable to COTS software procurement
Cannot compete against established SaaS PM software vendors on pricing
No SaaS infrastructure for ongoing support, updates, and patches over 8 years

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: This opportunity does not align with Horus Technology's documented strategic capabilities or market positioning. The company is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner focused on AI/ML, document automation, and cloud-native development for regulated and government-facing organizations. This solicitation requires a mature construction project management software vendor with a decade of public agency experience. Pursuing this opportunity would divert resources from the company's core competencies without building relevant portfolio value. The KB contains no strategic plans indicating expansion into construction PM software. The only potential strategic value would be monitoring Sacramento County for future IT modernization or AI/ML opportunities that match Horus's actual capabilities.
gaps: Construction PM software is outside company's core competency and strategic direction
No documented strategic plan to enter the construction PM software market
Pursuit would divert resources from AI/ML and document automation focus areas
No portfolio-building value in an industry the company does not serve

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: There is no incumbent. The solicitation explicitly states the Department does not currently use a project management system and relies on Excel spreadsheets and Crystal Reports. This is a new capability acquisition, not a recompete. However, while the absence of an incumbent is favorable in general, it does not benefit Horus because the company cannot meet the minimum qualifications regardless of incumbent status. The competitive field consists of established construction PM software vendors (Procore, e-Builder, Kahua, PMWeb, Oracle Primavera) who will compete aggressively for this greenfield opportunity.

data.summaryJustification: This is a clear NO_GO. Horus Technology is an AWS consulting/AI/ML firm that does not offer a commercial project management software product for construction and capital improvement planning. The solicitation has a pass/fail minimum qualification requiring 10 years of continuous experience providing PM software services to public agencies — Horus cannot meet this threshold and would be disqualified before evaluation. No past performance exists in this domain. The pricing module confirmed a fundamental industry mismatch with price confidence of 10. Additionally, the submission deadline of April 7, 2026 has already passed (today is April 19, 2026), making submission impossible. The only favorable factor is the absence of an incumbent, which is irrelevant given the disqualifying gaps.
blockers: Submission deadline has passed (April 7, 2026 — today is April 19, 2026)
Company does not offer a COTS project management software product for construction/CIP
Cannot meet pass/fail minimum qualification of 10 years continuous PM software experience with public agencies
No past performance in project management software, construction management, or airport operations
Fundamental industry mismatch — AI/ML consulting firm vs. construction PM software vendor
data.decisionRationale: NO_GO: Multiple critical disqualifiers prevent pursuit. (1) EXPIRED DEADLINE: The proposal due date of April 7, 2026 has passed — today is April 19, 2026, making submission impossible. (2) FUNDAMENTAL INDUSTRY MISMATCH: Horus is an AI/ML and document automation firm; this RFP requires a mature COTS construction project management software product. (3) PASS/FAIL DISQUALIFIER: The RFP mandates 10 years of continuous experience providing PM software to public agencies — Horus does not meet this minimum qualification and would be rejected before scoring. (4) NO PAST PERFORMANCE: Zero relevant projects in the database for PM software, construction management, or airport operations. (5) NO PRODUCT: Horus does not have a PM software product to license, deploy, or support over the 8-year contract term. Even without the expired deadline, this opportunity should not be pursued.

confidenceDrivers.factor: No past performance in PM software or construction management
Fundamental industry mismatch — AI/ML firm vs. construction PM software vendor
Pass/fail minimum qualification (10 years PM software experience) cannot be met
No COTS PM software product to offer
No incumbent — new requirement (favorable but irrelevant given disqualifiers)
Pricing module confirmed mismatch with price confidence of 10

data.summary: The Sacramento County Department of Airports is seeking a qualified vendor to provide a comprehensive cloud-based project management software solution for managing active construction projects and Capital Improvement Planning across its four airport facilities, including Sacramento International Airport. The solution must include software licensing, implementation, customization, system integration with existing ERP and GIS systems, training, ongoing technical support, and custom reporting to replace the current Excel-based tracking process. The contractor must have a minimum of ten years of experience providing project management software services to public agencies.
data.periodOfPerformance: Initial 4-year term with two 2-year extension options, for a possible total of 8 years
data.title: Project Management Software Services for Sacramento County Department of Airports
